message:
refactor(cli): use pattern-overlap for reservation conflict detection and improve doctor error classification

Reservation conflict detection (lib.rs):
- Replace per-path SQL queries with a single upfront query that fetches
  all active reservations for the project, then filter conflicts in
  Rust using reservation_patterns_overlap() backed by the cached
  CompiledPattern. This eliminates O(n) database round-trips during
  reservation creation and produces more accurate overlap detection
  that handles glob-vs-glob intersections correctly.
- Add cli_reservation_path_filter_matches() and
  cli_reservation_target_matches() helpers for consistent filter logic
  across list/release/extend commands

Doctor error classification (lib.rs):
- Reorder snapshot-conflict and recovery-error checks ahead of
  lock-error checks in the doctor health probe fallback paths. This
  prevents transient WAL snapshot conflicts from being misclassified
  as permanent "resource busy" errors, allowing the doctor to correctly
  retry or report the actual issue.

Other CLI changes:
- e2e_runner.rs: expanded E2E test runner with additional scenarios
- legacy.rs: updated legacy migration CLI paths
- robot.rs: expanded robot mode doctor integration
- integration_runs.rs: updated integration test expectations

Server:
- tui_poller.rs: expanded TUI poll loop with additional status tracking

Tools:
- build_slots.rs: minor formatting
- products.rs: minor formatting
- reservation_index.rs: minor formatting
- resources.rs: line-break long bindings

Test fixtures:
- Updated doctor help text and golden snapshot fixtures to reflect
  new doctor capabilities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+  archive-scan       Audit archive hygiene without mutating the mailbox archive
